evals.prompt: Scan my inbox for PLM-bound emails this week
evals.expected_output: Runs a folder-aware scan of Inbox + Sent Items driven by the run-time vendor/partner/contact lexicon (not a static sender list), classifies each PLM-relevant email into Flows A-G or I, runs the non-PLM signal scan in parallel (Quality/Regulatory/Margin/Intel/Founder), and presents the full PLM Triage Report format (URGENT, PURCHASE ORDERS, BATCH/COA LOGS, VENDOR UPDATES, FORMULA APPROVALS, TEST RESULTS, INVOICES, VENDOR INVOICES, COMPONENT/BOM UPDATES, then QUALITY/REGULATORY/MARGIN cross-flags, then outlook-asana-bridge cross-flags) ending in 'Which should I push?' — without committing any PLM writes.
expectations: The output searches or reasons about both Inbox and Sent Items, not just Inbox
Emails are classified into named flows (A through G, or I) rather than left unclassified
The non-PLM signal scan runs alongside the PLM classification (e.g. failing tests, Pedrero artifacts, cost changes get their own cross-flag section)
The output uses the PLM Triage Report section structure
No PLM INSERT/UPDATE is claimed as already committed — the output stages proposed writes for plm-assistant and waits for confirmation

evals.prompt: The KDC-One PO ack just came in — update PO #100336 in PLM
evals.expected_output: Finds the inbound KDC-One PO acknowledgment email, identifies this as Flow A (inbound = UPDATE, not INSERT), extracts the status/ship-date change, proposes the UPDATE on purchase_orders with a current-value → new-value preview (per the confirmation rules table — PLM UPDATE requires showing current → new value and confirming before handoff to plm-assistant), and notes the Asana sync-back comment that would post to the related PD task after the write commits.
expectations: The proposal is an UPDATE on the existing PO, not an INSERT of a new PO record
The preview shows a current value → new value comparison rather than just the new value alone
The output states the write will be staged and handed to plm-assistant rather than committed directly via execute_sql
An Asana sync-back comment on the related PD task is mentioned as a follow-on step
The skill does not claim to have created a new Asana task (that is outlook-asana-bridge's job, not this skill's)
